title: Beyond Words
anchor_summary: A single-player word puzzle roguelite built around Scrabble-like tile placement, run-based upgrades, heavy combo/synergy building, shifting board rules, boss challenges, and optional score/time-attack pressure.

similar_games.title: Letterlike
similar_games.why_similar: The clearest direct comp: a roguelike word game centered on letter tiles, run-based progression, shop/upgrades, and score-chasing strategic word play.

similar_games.title: Letter Quest: Grimm's Journey
similar_games.why_similar: Word-building is tied to combat encounters, upgrades, and perk-driven progression, making it a strong match for Beyond Words' wordplay-plus-buildcraft loop.

similar_games.title: Bookworm Adventures Deluxe
similar_games.why_similar: Another recognizable word-forming game where letters fuel tactical outcomes and progression, emphasizing long-form strategic word choice rather than pure crossword puzzling.

similar_games.title: SpellTower
similar_games.why_similar: A high-score word puzzle built around board management, planning, and pressure from changing board states, though it is less roguelite and less build-focused.

similar_games.title: Balatro
similar_games.why_similar: Not a word game, but very comparable in its run-based structure, escalating score targets, absurd modifier synergies, and combo-first puzzle-strategy mindset.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Store and aggregator text consistently describe a word-based strategic puzzle roguelite built around letter placement, combo building, modifiers, upgrades, seeded runs, time attacks, and boss challenges. The core loop is solving evolving word boards through tactical planning and synergy construction rather than action, simulation, or hidden-object play. Strong signals support a run-based word/puzzle strategy identity: 'roguelike strategy and word-crafting,' 'every placement matters,' 'over 300 unique modifiers and abilities,' 'each board is a unique puzzle,' 'boss challenges,' and optional time-attack/scored mastery modes. Combat is absent; challenge comes from puzzle optimization and buildcraft across repeated runs.

current_live_neighbors.title: Spider: Rite of the Shrouded Moon

title: The Occultist
anchor_summary: First-person single-player survival horror built around tense exploration, stealthy enemy avoidance, environmental interaction via a special tool, and a narrative investigation into supernatural events rather than direct combat.

similar_games.title: Amnesia: The Dark Descent
similar_games.why_similar: First-person survival horror centered on exploration, puzzle-solving, and avoiding threats instead of fighting them.

similar_games.title: Outlast
similar_games.why_similar: Powerless first-person horror with stealth, hiding, chase sequences, and a story-driven trek through hostile supernatural territory.

similar_games.title: SOMA
similar_games.why_similar: Narrative-first first-person horror with light puzzles, environmental storytelling, and survival through evasion rather than combat.

similar_games.title: Visage
similar_games.why_similar: Slow-burn first-person psychological/survival horror focused on unsettling exploration, item-based progression, and supernatural encounters.

similar_games.title: MADiSON
similar_games.why_similar: First-person horror built around using a special investigation tool to interact with paranormal phenomena and solve progression puzzles.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Official store and metadata evidence consistently describe The Occultist as a first-person narrative thriller with explicit 'survival horror' framing, tense exploration, supernatural threats, and stealth-focused avoidance rather than direct combat. The player explores an abandoned island, investigates a father's disappearance and occult rituals, uses a mystic pendulum to interact with and modify the environment, and must 'hide and sneak' because 'Alan always avoids direct combat.' That combination strongly supports survival_horror as the core gameplay identity, with psychological_horror and stealth_action_adventure as secondary overlaps.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: First-person survival horror centered on exploration, puzzle-solving, and avoiding threats instead of fighting them.

title: Samson
anchor_summary: Open-world action game built around street brawling plus aggressive car chases/weaponized driving, with mission-based city traversal, character upgrades, and pressure-driven progression.

similar_games.title: Sleeping Dogs
similar_games.why_similar: Open-world crime action centered on hand-to-hand combat, city traversal, driving between missions, and story-driven street-level progression.

similar_games.title: Mad Max
similar_games.why_similar: Blends brutal melee combat with vehicle combat and chases, upgrading both the hero and car as core progression.

similar_games.title: Wheelman
similar_games.why_similar: Action-heavy urban driving game where cars are used offensively and missions pivot between on-foot action and high-speed escapes.

similar_games.title: Mafia III
similar_games.why_similar: Story-driven open-world crime structure with mission pressure, driving-heavy city navigation, and frequent shootout/brawl encounters.

similar_games.title: Driver: San Francisco
similar_games.why_similar: Best match on the chase-and-collision side: arcade-style city driving built around pursuit, ramming, and mission-based urban action.

title: Starfield - Terran Armada
anchor_summary: Story DLC for Starfield, inheriting its core identity as a single-player Bethesda-style first/third-person open-world space RPG with faction questing, loot, shooting, exploration, character progression, and gear upgrades.

similar_games.title: Starfield
similar_games.why_similar: Terran Armada is additive story DLC built directly on Starfield’s open-world space RPG, shooting, questing, and progression systems.

similar_games.title: The Outer Worlds
similar_games.why_similar: Single-player sci-fi first-person RPG centered on hub-based exploration, gunplay, dialogue choices, companions, and quest-driven progression.

similar_games.title: Fallout 4
similar_games.why_similar: Bethesda-style open-world action RPG with real-time shooting, looting, crafting, faction quests, and character build progression.

similar_games.title: Cyberpunk 2077
similar_games.why_similar: First-person RPG/shooter focused on questlines, character builds, gear upgrades, and narrative-driven combat encounters.

similar_games.title: Mass Effect: Andromeda
similar_games.why_similar: Single-player sci-fi action RPG with planetary exploration, squad-based combat, progression trees, and story-focused mission structure.

payload.anchor_summary: Story DLC for Starfield, inheriting its core identity as a single-player Bethesda-style first/third-person open-world space RPG with faction questing, loot, shooting, exploration, character progression, and gear upgrades.

similar_games.title: Starfield
similar_games.why_similar: Terran Armada is additive story DLC built directly on Starfield’s open-world space RPG, shooting, questing, and progression systems.

similar_games.title: The Outer Worlds

similar_games.title: Mass Effect: Andromeda
similar_games.why_similar: Single-player sci-fi action RPG with planetary exploration, squad-based combat, progression trees, and story-focused mission structure.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: The title is explicitly a story DLC for Starfield, and its own description is additive rather than genre-altering: new locations, technologies, weapons, and story combat against a hostile faction. Per DLC guidance, the parent game's stored evidence is the primary basis. Starfield's parent description strongly establishes a Bethesda-style open-world sci-fi RPG built around character creation, freeform exploration across planets, faction and side quests, dialogue-driven roleplay, ship piloting/combat, loot/crafting, outpost building, and long-form single-player progression. That aligns best with western_narrative_rpg rather than a pure shooter or action-adventure archetype. The DLC copy does not contradict that core gameplay identity; it appears to extend the same quest-driven open-world campaign structure with additional combat content and exploration spaces.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Terran Armada is additive story DLC built directly on Starfield’s open-world space RPG, shooting, questing, and progression systems.
Single-player sci-fi first-person RPG centered on hub-based exploration, gunplay, dialogue choices, companions, and quest-driven progression.
Bethesda-style open-world action RPG with real-time shooting, looting, crafting, faction quests, and character build progression.
First-person RPG/shooter focused on questlines, character builds, gear upgrades, and narrative-driven combat encounters.
Single-player sci-fi action RPG with planetary exploration, squad-based combat, progression trees, and story-focused mission structure.

title: Machine Child
anchor_summary: A choice-driven daughter-raising sim focused on scheduling activities, building affection, unlocking story events and outfits, and steering a girl toward one of many possible endings.

similar_games.title: Princess Maker 2 Refine
similar_games.why_similar: Foundational daughter-raising sim built around training stats, managing her schedule, relationship events, and branching life outcomes.

similar_games.title: Volcano Princess
similar_games.why_similar: Modern parent-sim where you raise a daughter through stat growth, lessons, social events, dress-up, and many ending paths.

similar_games.title: Long Live the Queen
similar_games.why_similar: Choice-heavy stat-management game where weekly planning and event decisions determine narrative outcomes and endings.

similar_games.title: Ciel Fledge: A Daughter Raising Simulator
similar_games.why_similar: Directly comparable raising sim centered on nurturing a girl, balancing activities, and shaping her future through branching events.

similar_games.title: Chinese Parents
similar_games.why_similar: Life-raising simulation focused on guided development, schedule choices, stat growth, and multiple future trajectories, though with a broader family-life framing.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently present Machine Child as a girl-raising/nurturing simulation centered on guiding a daughter through life in a town, managing choices across 700+ story events, building affection, selecting activities and outfits, and reaching 22 different endings. The core loop is non-combat, relationship- and choice-driven, with strong branching life-path outcomes and a single-player replayable structure. That aligns best with life_sim. Some presentation details like camera/perspective are not evidenced, so those fields are left blank.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Foundational daughter-raising sim built around training stats, managing her schedule, relationship events, and branching life outcomes.
